name: GT-positive-monotone
source: Idel 2013, refs/idel-2013/idel-2013.md:347 (Definition 2.1), VERBATIM: "T is called positive, if for all  $A \ge 0$ ,  $T(A) \ge 0$ . In particular, a positive map is Hermitian". Hence positivity + linearity give monotonicity: A<=B (i.e. B-A>=0) implies Phi(B-A)>=0 i.e. Phi(A)<=Phi(B). Instantiated at Phi unital positive on B(H)_sa.
